financial.note: AMATA maintains significant debt facilities for land development and utility infrastructure; rising rates increase interest expense and CAPEX costs.
Provides working capital and project finance for industrial estate expansions in the EEC and regional markets.

supply_chain.note: Joint venture via Amata B.Grimm Power; reliable power supply is a core utility revenue driver and critical for tenant acquisition.
Direct fuel cost for utility operations; natural gas price fluctuations through PTT NGD affect industrial service margins.
Strategic partner for power infrastructure in the Amata Smart and Eco City (Laos) expansion.

competitors.note: Direct rival in Thailand's EEC; competition for global FDI (Data Centers, EV manufacturers) pressures land pricing.
Competes for Japanese manufacturing tenants and electronic sector relocation.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Primary driver for land sales; sensitive to the 'China Plus One' strategy and geopolitical relocation trends.
A weaker THB makes Thai industrial land more attractive to foreign investors, though it increases the cost of imported infrastructure tech.
Directly impacts the cost of funding for high-CAPEX land development and the valuation of the group's REIT (AMATAR).
